leaderRationale: Pushback is required because reviewer-codex-r0 issued a reject with blocking and major findings, and the self-check independently confirms the blocking canonical-output failure. Under the approved review-process architecture, blocking/major findings are material, freeze is not allowed while they remain unresolved, and arbitration cannot be used on them.
unresolved: Canonical output paths are not defined as one authoritative contract: <outputs> and Step 5 use slug/basename forms while the required canonical files live under tools/agent-role-builder/role/, and self-check already flags all four required outputs as missing.

Artifact lifecycle and terminal-state behavior are not mechanically derivable: the canonical directory is unnamed, decision-log and board-summary preserve/append/replace behavior is undefined, and resume_required has no explicit trigger distinct from pushback or blocked.

Governance authority order conflicts with approved architecture: the role says it is subordinate to static docs instead of treating live COO/memory-engine governance as primary and docs as reference evidence.

Freeze and arbitration semantics conflict with the approved review process: the draft requires every reviewer to approve, omits the conditional/frozen_with_conditions path for minor-only outcomes, and introduces 'arbitrate after 2+ consecutive splits' even though arbitration is minor-only and cannot override material pushback.
The contract intent is run-specific/bootstrap-specific rather than a stable standing mission for the role package.

conceptual_groups.summary: Canonical artifact paths and lifecycle are not defined in a single authoritative, testable way.

findings.description: The canonical artifacts in <outputs> are listed only as slug templates like '<slug>-role.md', while the package already expects concrete canonical paths under 'tools/agent-role-builder/role/'. The supplied self-check proves this mismatch by flagging all four required canonical outputs as missing.

Step 5 says artifacts are promoted to a 'canonical directory', but that directory is never named, and the contract summary's package_files also uses basename-only paths. That leaves no single path authority for promotion or validation.

Decision log and board summary lifecycle is ambiguous. Step 5 says they are always written, <outputs> describes them as canonical artifacts promoted on freeze, and the guardrails require history preservation across update/fix operations, but append-vs-replace behavior is never defined.
The terminal states are listed, but 'resume_required' has no explicit trigger condition distinct from pushback or blocked, so the artifact set for each terminal outcome is not mechanically derivable.

conceptual_groups.redesign_guidance: Make canonical tool-relative paths the single authority across <outputs> and the contract, explicitly separate canonical artifacts from run-scoped evidence, and define lifecycle rules per artifact and per terminal state, including exact preserve/append/replace behavior.

conceptual_groups.summary: Governance authority and freeze semantics conflict with the approved architecture and introduce invented operating rules.

findings.description: The authority section makes the role 'Subordinate to' static docs, but bootstrap and architecture say operative governance is loaded by the COO from the memory engine and docs are scaffolding/reference. This blurs reference evidence with runtime authority.

The draft says to freeze only when every reviewer approves, but the approved review process explicitly allows freeze when reviewers approve or go conditional after minor fixes, and it defines 'frozen_with_conditions' for minor-item arbitration outcomes.

The Step 4 rule 'Arbitrate after 2+ consecutive splits' is not the approved arbitration rule. The approved architecture allows arbitration only for minor/suggestion disagreement and never to override a reject verdict or material pushback.
The contract summary intent is run-specific ('Resume and finalize the agent-role-builder self-role...') instead of role-authoritative. That leaks bootstrap history into the stable role contract.

conceptual_groups.redesign_guidance: Define authority order explicitly: live COO/memory-engine governance first, request/contract next, static docs as reference evidence only. Then collapse freeze/arbitration language to one canonical rule that matches the approved review-process architecture.

conceptual_groups.summary: Scope boundaries are duplicated and therefore less machine-checkable than they should be.

findings.description: 'Application workflow creation' appears twice in the not-in-scope list with different wording.
Application runtime orchestration is excluded twice with overlapping carve-outs, so the board-review exception reads like an afterthought instead of one clear boundary.

conceptual_groups.redesign_guidance: Deduplicate the exclusions and state the board-review carve-out once in a single explicit sentence so the in-scope boundary can be validated mechanically.

residual_risks: Step 3 claims broad self-check coverage such as lifecycle consistency, but the evidence shown here only demonstrates path failures; that wording may still overstate what the validator actually proves.
Without one source of truth for canonical paths, future drafts can keep drifting between <outputs>, package_files, and self-check expectations.
If terminal-state predicates remain implicit, different runs may classify the same failure as pushback, blocked, or resume_required and produce different artifact sets.
strengths: The draft includes the full required tag set.
The core role mission is correctly centered on governed agent-role package creation, update, and repair.
Several guardrails are directionally strong: no invented semantics, no authority expansion beyond request, provenance attachment, and reviewer-pair composition.

conceptual_groups.summary: Output paths in <outputs> use relative slug-only names; self-check expects fully-qualified paths under tools/agent-role-builder/role/

findings.description: Canonical artifact entries (<slug>-role.md, <slug>-role-contract.json, etc.) omit the canonical directory path prefix. Self-check reports all four as MISSING_REQUIRED_OUTPUT because it cannot resolve them to disk paths.

conceptual_groups.redesign_guidance: Expand each canonical artifact entry to include the canonical path, e.g. 'tools/agent-role-builder/role/<slug>-role.md -- Canonical: ...'. Alternatively, add a preamble sentence stating 'All canonical artifacts are written to tools/agent-role-builder/role/' so the self-check resolver has an unambiguous base path.

conceptual_groups.summary: Duplicate 'Application workflow creation' entry in <scope> Not-in-scope list

findings.description: 'Application workflow creation' appears twice in the Not-in-scope bullet list.

conceptual_groups.summary: Contract intent field describes a resume operation, not the standing role purpose

findings.description: contract.intent reads 'Resume and finalize the agent-role-builder self-role using improved review process...' — this describes a one-time run, not the permanent role purpose. Future consumers of the contract will read intent as the tool's standing mission.

conceptual_groups.redesign_guidance: Set intent to a timeless mission statement, e.g. 'Create, update, and fix agent role packages through governed multi-LLM review, producing frozen packages only when no material pushback remains.' Move the resume-specific note to a separate run_context or notes field if needed.

residual_risks: Self-check path resolution logic may continue to flag MISSING_REQUIRED_OUTPUT if canonical base path is added as prose rather than structured metadata — verify self-check actually parses the updated form.

Split-verdict arbitration trigger ('after 2+ consecutive splits') is defined in <steps> but not reflected in governance config — a future runner reading only the contract will not know the arbitration threshold.
strengths: Authority boundaries are crisp and non-overlapping with llm-tool-builder and COO controller.
Freeze condition is stated twice (guardrails + step 4) in consistent language — no ambiguity about what 'frozen' means.
Artifact lifecycle is well-structured: canonical vs. evidence vs. internal categories are clearly separated with promotion semantics.
Guardrail against inventing missing semantics ('return pushback instead') directly closes a common LLM failure mode.
Resume-package pattern preserves run continuity without requiring a full restart — good operational hygiene.
